_comment: Daily Gauntlet content. One theme threads all five stages (Wordle -> Unearth -> Spelling Bee -> Connections -> Crossword). Generated by make_gauntlet.py — edit the generator, not this file.

days.intro: The garden is just waking up. Six letters are buried in the soil — dig them out, then enrich the bed to find the seventh.

wordle.handoff: Six letters — G A R D E N. A hive flower holds seven. Enrich the bed to find the last.

unearth.intro: A garden is only as good as its soil. Dig up two things — but the hive has room for just one more letter. You’ll choose which.

puzzles.clue: What you turn over with a spade before planting
Spongy dark matter you mix in to feed the bed

unearth.choicePrompt: Two letters unearthed — but only one fits the flower. Plant the one you think grows the most words.

bee._note: letters = the 6 Wordle letters + the planted bonus; words span BOTH choices and the runtime keeps only those buildable from the chosen seven. Pangrams (all 7) are detected at runtime. Auto-derived by make_gauntlet.py.
bee.handoff: The words you coaxed up are scattered through the beds below. Sort them into their plots.

connections.handoff: Four plots, four words each. The last of them tangle together in the knot at the gate.

across.1: What the gardener pours into a tended plot
across.6: Glow left in the pile of burning autumn leaves
across.7: Neglect, which an unwatered bed suffers
across.8: Sticky sap that weeps from a pine
across.9: The cottage-garden look that’s in fashion this year

down.1: Center of an artichoke, or of a lettuce
down.2: Smoldering remnant in the fire pit
down.3: To over-prune to the point of harm
down.4: Pine pitch, again from the top
down.5: A drift, or current direction of the breeze

crossword.finale: The garden is in full bloom. You grew the whole thing from six buried letters.

days.intro: Something’s prowling the house today. Coax six letters out of hiding, then earn the seventh.

wordle.handoff: C A T N I P. One more letter and the flower’s full — what does the cat drag in?

unearth.intro: Cats bring you things whether you want them or not. Sort out what each one is.

puzzles.clue: Soft feet that knead your lap
The sound at 5 a.m. demanding breakfast

unearth.choicePrompt: Two letters batted under the couch — only one fits the flower. Plant the one that grows the most words.

bee._note: letters = the 6 Wordle letters + the planted bonus; words span BOTH choices and the runtime keeps only those buildable from the chosen seven. Pangrams (all 7) are detected at runtime. Auto-derived by make_gauntlet.py.
bee.handoff: Round up the words like strays and sort them into four baskets.

connections.handoff: The strays settle into the last knot by the windowsill.

across.8: Pulling a rabbit from a hat
across.9: Wintry mix of rain and ice

crossword.finale: The cat curls up, satisfied. You earned every letter of its approval.

days.intro: Long days, no plans. Dig six letters out of the warm sand and chase down the seventh.

wordle.handoff: S U N D A E. The flower wants one more — what makes a perfect beach day?

unearth.intro: Two things every summer day needs. Unscramble what they are.

puzzles.clue: What the midday sun beats down in waves
Ride the waves on a board

unearth.choicePrompt: Two letters washed up — only one fits the flower. Plant the one that grows the most words.

bee.handoff: Scoop the words up like ice cream and sort them into four cones.

connections.handoff: The last words drift together like a tide pool at the gate.

across.1: Drum with a buzzing wire, or a trap
across.7: Little symbols you tap on a phone
across.9: Place to book a room for the night

crossword.finale: Sun’s going down on a perfect day — and you saw it all the way through.

days.intro: Kickoff. Win six letters off the pitch, then earn the one that completes the squad.

wordle.handoff: S T R I K E. The flower needs one more player — sub them on.

unearth.intro: Two things you can’t play without. Sort out what they are.

puzzles.clue: What every striker is trying to score
What the ball hits when it goes in (plural)

down.3: Photo, or how others see you
down.5: A diary post, or a way in

crossword.finale: Full time, and the result was never in doubt. Well played.

wordle.handoff: S H A K E R. One more ingredient and the flower’s complete — what finishes the drink?

unearth.intro: Two things behind every good bar. Sort out what they are.

A wedge of this twists over the rim

unearth.choicePrompt: Two letters on the rail — only one goes in. Pour the one that grows the most words.

puzzles.clue: A catchy melody you can hum

days.intro: Dive in. Bring six letters up from the deep, then surface with the seventh.

wordle.handoff: I S L A N D. One more and the flower’s whole — what rolls in from the deep?

unearth.intro: Two things the sea keeps doing. Sort out what they are.

puzzles.clue: It rises and falls twice a day
White seabird that steals your chips

unearth.choicePrompt: Two letters in the surf — only one washes ashore. Plant the one that grows the most words.

bee._note: letters = the 6 Wordle letters + the planted bonus; words span BOTH choices and the runtime keeps only those buildable from the chosen seven. Pangrams (all 7) are detected at runtime. Auto-derived by make_gauntlet.py.

connections.handoff: The last four pull together in the knot beneath the waves.

days.intro: Preheat the oven. Knead six letters out of the dough, then fold in the seventh.

wordle.handoff: B A K I N G. One more and the flower rises — what do you fold in?

unearth.intro: Two things every recipe starts with. Sort out what they are.

puzzles.clue: Crack a couple of these into the bowl
Sift a cup of this, the base of it all

unearth.choicePrompt: Two letters on the counter — only one folds in. Plant the one that grows the most words.

bee._note: letters = the 6 Wordle letters + the planted bonus; words span BOTH choices and the runtime keeps only those buildable from the chosen seven. Pangrams (all 7) are detected at runtime. Auto-derived by make_gauntlet.py.

connections.handoff: The last four come together in the knot cooling on the rack.

across.6: One who gives stars or scores

crossword.finale: Ding — it’s done, and it rose perfectly. You baked it from scratch.
